---
title: Microsoft Defender for Cloud Apps — Actions (Reserved Slot) — Adapter Technical
  Specification
doc-type: ats
adapter-id: defender-for-cloud-apps-actions
adapter-class: modernization
mission-class: integration
status: reserved
phase: phase-planning
gcc-boundary: gcc-moderate
canon-source: src/uiao/canon/modernization-registry.yaml
derived-from: uiao/canon (sync_canon.py)
generated: '2026-05-07'
---

# Microsoft Defender for Cloud Apps — Actions (Reserved Slot) — Adapter Technical Specification

::: {.callout-note}
## Canon-derived document

**Status:** `reserved` · **Class:** `modernization` · **Mission:** `integration` · **Phase:** `phase-planning`

**Canon source:** `canon/modernization-registry.yaml` (propagated by `uiao/tools/sync_canon.py`).

The YAML frontmatter and this banner are regenerated from canon on every sync. **Do not hand-edit.** Author new material only below the `## Overview` heading.
:::

## Overview

The Microsoft Defender for Cloud Apps — Actions Adapter is the **CASB enforcement actuator** in the UIAO substrate. As a modernization-class integration adapter, it performs the change-making operations on the Microsoft CASB surface — OAuth app governance (revoke / restrict consent), session-control policy writes, and Conditional Access App Control bindings — that turn the read-only observations from [`defender-for-cloud-apps`](../defender-for-cloud-apps/defender-for-cloud-apps.html) into enforced posture.

The dual-axis split is intentional: the conformance entry observes Shadow IT, OAuth inventory, app risk, and session analytics; this entry acts on those observations. Per ADR-049 §Decision 1, this is the canonical pattern for Microsoft surfaces that have both read-only and change-making operational planes.

Three primary functions:

1. **OAuth app governance.** Revokes or restricts user consent to third-party OAuth apps based on canon-defined risk thresholds and app-classification rules. Phantom revocations (apps that are unknown to canon but flagged as high-risk by Defender) surface as findings; only canon-authorized actions auto-apply.
2. **Session-control policy authoring.** Writes Defender for Cloud Apps session policies that govern in-session actions (download blocking, cut/copy/paste restrictions, file-upload scanning) for OrgPath-keyed user populations and sanctioned-app combinations.
3. **Conditional Access App Control binding.** Provisions the Conditional Access policies that route traffic for governed apps through the CASB session-control reverse proxy. Bindings reference [`entra-dynamic-groups`](../entra-dynamic-groups/entra-dynamic-groups.html) OrgTree-* groups so the population subject to session control tracks OrgPath assignment automatically.

**Implementation:** Reserved slot per ADR-049. Activation requires a per-adapter ADR modeled on ADR-035.

## Scope

Target surfaces / subsystems: `oauth-app-governance`, `session-control-policies`, `conditional-access-app-control`

**Reads:** UIAO_154 / UIAO_155 governance canon (which OAuth governance actions are pre-authorized), the canon-defined CASB session-control policy library, and the live Defender for Cloud Apps configuration via the Defender APIs. Cross-references the read-only inventory emitted by [`defender-for-cloud-apps`](../defender-for-cloud-apps/defender-for-cloud-apps.html) to identify governance candidates.

**Emits:** `mcas-policy-manifest.json` (planned policy / binding changes with per-row provenance back to canon), `mcas-action-audit.json` (post-apply audit trail with one provenance record per OAuth revocation / policy write / CA binding change), and skipped-manual findings for actions that fall outside canon authorization.

**Does NOT:** observe or score apps (that's [`defender-for-cloud-apps`](../defender-for-cloud-apps/defender-for-cloud-apps.html)'s role), modify app catalog metadata, sanction or unsanction apps without canon authority, or auto-revoke OAuth consents whose blast radius is unknown — phantom revocations surface as findings rather than executing. Apply paths are gated on `dry_run=False`; broad-blast-radius CA App Control changes additionally require human approval through UIAO_155.

## Controls

NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5 controls this adapter supports: `AC-3`, `AC-6`, `AC-17`, `AU-2`

| Control | Role | Adapter capability |
|---|---|---|
| **AC-3** Access Enforcement | Primary | Conditional Access App Control bindings + session-control policies are the enforcement edge for SaaS access decisions. |
| **AC-6** Least Privilege | Primary | OAuth app governance revokes or restricts over-permissioned third-party app consents — the change-making half of the least-privilege review that the conformance side observes. |
| **AC-17** Remote Access | Supporting | Session-control policies govern in-session actions for remote / BYOD access to sanctioned SaaS apps. |
| **AU-2** Event Logging | Supporting | Every policy / consent / binding change emits an audit record into the canonical evidence stream. |

## Operational profile

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Runtime | `TBD` |
| Runtime pin | `TBD` |
| Runner class | `TBD` |
| Tenancy | `per-customer` |
| Evidence class | `baseline` |
| Retention | `3` year(s) |

## Canon invariants

- `gcc-boundary: gcc-moderate`
- `ssot-mutation: never`
- `certificate-anchored: true`
- `object-identity-only: true`

## Notes from canon

Reserved slot per ADR-049. Activation requires a per-adapter ADR modeled on ADR-035. The split across both registries ([`defender-for-cloud-apps`](../defender-for-cloud-apps/defender-for-cloud-apps.html) in conformance, this entry in modernization) is the precedent set by ADR-049 §Decision 1 for Microsoft surfaces that have both a read-only and a change-making axis.

ZTMM pillars: applications-and-workloads, data.

## References

- UIAO-CANON-003
- ADR-049 — Reserved-slot allocation framework + dual-axis split rationale

---

*Generated by `uiao/tools/sync_canon.py`. See `uiao/ARCHITECTURE.md` §4 for the cross-repo sync contract. See `uiao-docs/_quarto.yml` for rendering configuration.*
